evals.prompt: Analyze example.com for topical authority and content gaps, then recommend the next five articles and save the report in the default location.
evals.expected_output: Retrieves 200 target keywords, discovers five competitors, runs pairwise competitor-only gap analysis plus the top competitor keyword call, clusters the evidence, calculates the Content Score, and writes the dated report under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: The target ranked-keywords call uses limit 200 and organic results.
Exactly five discovered competitors are retained and each receives a competitor-only domain-intersection call.
The Content Score uses the requested strong-cluster, missing-cluster, and normalized top-quartile position components.

evals.prompt: Find our strongest and missing content topics and tell me what to publish next.
evals.expected_output: Asks for the missing target domain and waits without making a billable call.
assertions: The workflow asks for the target domain and waits.
No DataForSEO call or report is produced before a valid domain is supplied.

evals.prompt: Run content suggestions for https://www.example.org/store against rival-one.test, rival-two.test, and rival-three.test in Germany, language de, and save it under ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Normalizes the domain, uses the supplied competitors and market, performs the supported pairwise content-gap workflow, creates no more than 15 clusters, and writes a detailed report below the requested root.
assertions: The target is normalized to example.org and competitor discovery is skipped.
Germany, de, and the three supplied competitors are used consistently across MCP calls.
The analysis produces 8-15 clusters when the evidence supports that range and applies all four statuses deterministically.
Five article briefs include target and related keywords, intent evidence, prioritization metrics, and editorial word-count estimates.
